_doc: BWM Client Locks — machine-checkable contract for asap-pest-wildlife. Each assertion is a perceptual failure mode the team committed to never recur. Loaded by Brain's tools/bwm-client-locks-qa.py during PATCH and FULL BUILD. Drift from these locks = build failure. Source incidents: John's 2026-05-08 website-review email + Nehemiah's 2026-05-11 meeting (transcript at ~/Downloads/nehemiah-2026-05-11-transcript.md) + John's 2026-05-15 v8.1 review (over-removed ampersand + phone-tooth overlap).

required_strings.rationale: Sealing entry points is core to wildlife process — was replaced by 'Empathy' in v6, restored 2026-05-12 per client review.

Allaine 2026-05-18 flag: nav transparent overlapped content on scroll. Header MUST carry id="site-header" so main.js can toggle .is-scrolled (CSS in style.css adds bg-cream/95 + backdrop-blur). Removing the id silently regresses nav readability on scroll.
Allaine 2026-05-18: scroll-state JS must remain in main.js (else nav stays transparent forever). Lock guards both the CSS class hook and the toggle handler.

Robert 2026-05-18 flag (v8.4): the More tab's ampersand must sit BEFORE 'More' with negative margin-right (underlaps the M from the left), matching removeasap.com reference. The we-remove-amp class on the img anchors the structure; removing it regresses to the spaced-after layout that prompted the flag.

John Mukasa 2026-03-09 ask (v8.4 Phase 1 close-out): bee page must surface the HBR (Honey Bee Removal Operator) certification under the Georgia Structural Pest Control Act. This is ASAP's primary honey-bee differentiator versus generic pest spraying.
John Mukasa 2026-03-09 ask: legal statute name must appear verbatim per HBR licensure framing.
John Mukasa 2026-03-09 ask: bee page must call out ASAP's nonprofit-relocation partnership for live honey bee colonies.
John Mukasa 2026-03-09 ask (v8.4): bee-specific FAQ accordion (5 items) on the bee page. The id anchors the section for lock + future deep-linking from ads.

Robert 2026-05-18 (v8.6): the More Reviews button must land on ASAP's verified Google Business Profile (CID-based Maps URL), not a generic '/place/Name/@coords/' search that resolves to a search result instead of the GBP listing. CID 7456357551456980082 = hex 0x677a4db004e50c72 from the canonical Maps data hash for ASAP Pest & Wildlife Removal.
Same — applies to the About-page More Reviews button.

Robert 2026-05-18 (v8.6): wildlife card overlays (Armadillo, Bat, etc.) previously used 'opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100' with no mobile fallback — hover doesn't fire on touch so the animal descriptions were completely invisible on mobile. The new pattern always shows the overlay on mobile (opacity-100) and preserves hover-reveal on md+ (md:opacity-0 md:group-hover:opacity-100). Burn incident: bob 2026-05-18 marked 'optimize for Mobile View' addressed via v8.2 (closeout_ref bbf3100) when v8.2 didn't actually touch this overlay pattern.
Same — applies sitewide on the wildlife listing page.

required_strings.string: nmsdc-2024.png" alt="NMSDC Certified 2024" class="max-h-12 max-w-full object-contain invert opacity-70"
required_strings.rationale: Robert 2026-05-18 (v8.6): the NMSDC certified-2024 PNG is rendered in light gray on transparent background (authored for dark-bg context). Without the 'invert' filter (and 70% opacity to match neighboring monochrome logo weight), it renders gray-on-cream and is invisible on ASAP's cream partner band. v8.5 fixed five other partner SVGs by swapping fill to navy; PNG can't be edited the same way, so the CSS filter is load-bearing. Removing 'invert' regresses to invisible.

forbidden_strings.rationale: Robert 2026-05-18 (v8.5): the 'More Photos' button on About linked to ASAP's Google Maps business listing. ASAP is a mobile-service company with no retail location; sending visitors to a Maps page implies otherwise. Removed in v8.5. Lock guards against re-insertion.

Robert 2026-05-18 (v8.5): the 'Google Photos →' link on Wildlife had a text/destination mismatch (label said Google Photos, href pointed to Google Maps). Removed in v8.5. Lock guards both the broken label and re-insertion of any Google Photos CTA without a real photo album URL.

Robert 2026-05-18 (v8.6): logo-orange-tagline.png has the 'Your pest and wildlife extraordinaires' cursive baked into the PNG in #F3EFDD (cream) — contrast ratio ~1.02:1 on the white/cream header. Tagline was invisible on desktop and mobile. logo-orange.png is the same logo with the tagline rendered in dark navy and is the canonical header asset. Lock guards against reverting to the broken asset.

Robert 2026-05-18 (v8.6): the original More Reviews href was a generic Google Maps search ('/place/ASAP+Wildlife+Removal/@33.734354,-84.242248,10z/') that doesn't anchor to ASAP's verified GBP — it resolves to a Maps search result, not the business listing. Replaced with a CID-based URL. Lock guards against reverting to the generic-search form.
Same — applies to the About-page button.

forbidden_substring_in_svg_fill.rationale: Robert 2026-05-18 (v8.5): partner logos tps.svg, ccm.svg, cw.svg, gfl.svg, ksm.svg were authored with cream (#F2EDDC) fills for a dark-bg context. On ASAP's cream partner band the logos rendered as cream-on-cream and were invisible to visitors. Fills swapped to navy (#212936). Lock prevents future regression where a partner SVG ships with cream fill against the cream partner section.

required_assets.rationale: Item #5: All inner pages have the orange-tooth CTA banner. About was missing it pre-2026-05-12.
Item #9: Contact-form section uses the canonical monster, not the thin-mouth-line SVG.
Item #9: Contact-form section uses the canonical monster, not the thin-mouth-line SVG.
Homepage CTA banner shows the orange-tooth element.

Required asset for the 'More' tab decorative '&'. Layout history: v8.1 over-removed both ampersands; v8.2 restored the after-'More' instance; v8.4 (Robert 2026-05-18) moved it BEFORE 'More' with negative margin-right per removeasap.com reference (underlaps the M from the left, larger, opacity-80). Current authoritative structure is anchored by the we-remove-amp class — see required_strings entry below.

forbidden_assets.rationale: Item #9: This is the deprecated thin-mouth-line monster. Never use in contact sections.

required_class_on_h1.rationale: Item #1: Inner-page H1s use the hollowed-out style matching homepage Peace-of-Mind treatment.

min_pattern_count.rationale: Item #4: Homepage partner-logo grid has 9 logos in a normalized grid (was unaligned flex-wrap before 2026-05-12).
Item #8: Commercial page partner-logo grid (Walmart alignment was the trigger).
